title: Mellow Braised Turkish Lamb Stew
description: A charred and flaky Turkish lamb stew featuring sweet sherry, perfect for a weeknight dinner, and friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences by using no animal by-products other than lamb.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove from pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it starts to caramelize, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the sweet sherry, chicken broth, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the lamb pieces to the pot and bring the stew to a boil.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and let the stew cool slightly before serving.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
